title: Radiogrammitis parva
intro: Radiogrammitis parva, also known as the Small Grammitid fern, is a species of small to medium-sized fern native to the tropical forests of Southeast Asia. This plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and skin infections, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Radiogrammitis parva include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and sesquiterpenoids, which have been shown to exhibit antioxidant and anti-inflammatory activities. The plant is commonly used to make herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, as well as being incorporated into traditional remedies such as poultices and topical creams.
